_note: SQL-derived facts, reconfirmed serially against dhamma-pg (snapshot 194,710 passages, 2026-06-19). Every count here was produced by a GROUP BY / count query, not a search impression.

recall_ladder.strategy: naive surface string 'arahattaṁ pāpuṇi' (bar-niggahita glyph, as a non-specialist types it), ILIKE
recall_ladder.note: the CST corpus writes niggahita as the dotted-m glyph (ṃ), so the bar-m (ṁ) surface string finds almost nothing

recall_ladder.strategy: glyph-normalized + declension/sandhi (dotted-m ṃ; pāpuṇi/patto/pattā/āpādi), regex over the arahatta-attainment family
recall_ladder.note: the glyph normalization is the dominant recovery; +912 over rung 1

recall_ladder.strategy: concept/periphrasis union, name-independent (desanāpariyosāne, gāthāpariyosāne, anupādāya āsavehi cittaṃ vimucci, virajaṃ vītamalaṃ dhammacakkhuṃ udapādi, khīṇā jāti, aññaṃ byākāsi) across all markers
recall_ladder.note: the mass-awakening formula desanāpariyosāne is 0 in mula and 206 in attha, a commentarial narrative marker; +574 over rung 2

recall_ladder.strategy: false-positive purge (doctrinal/abstract uses of the markers set aside as non-events by the close read)
recall_ladder.note: the census reports 4,023 classified marker passages, of which 2,214 narrate a real event and 1,809 are doctrinal/abstract and were set aside; the 2,214 is the net event count after the purge

quotative_scan_mula.note: of the 299 mula events, 206 carry first-person self-narration markers (the Apadāna / Theragāthā 'I attained' verse); only 22 carry bhagavā etadavoca; 48 carry the redactor's evaṃ me sutaṃ / tena samayena frame

early_nikaya_prose_external.note: of the 33 early-canonical Nikāya-prose awakening events, external (non-Pāli) parallels exist for several, a link-level proxy for a pre-sectarian core of the bare awakening narration

apadana_buddhavamsa_external.note: link-level only; the Apadāna/Buddhavaṃsa works carry some external parallels at work level, but the genre is philologically late-canonical; container-vs-feature kept distinct, coded link-level
